name: Watchman Campground
location: Inside Zion National Park, near Visitor Center
highlights: The most immersive option — you wake up in the canyon
190 sites, some riverside
Walking distance to Visitor Center and shuttle
Excellent stargazing from campground
bookingWindow: Book at recreation.gov as soon as available — fills fast in peak season (Mar–Oct)
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Cold winter nights (25–35°F) — bring warm gear. Summer is hot — shade is limited.

name: Cable Mountain Lodge
location: Springdale, walking distance to park entrance and shuttle
highlights: Excellent location — walk to park entrance
Pool and hot tub
Best value in the rooted tier
Shuttle stop nearby
bookingWindow: 2–3 months ahead for peak season (Sep–Oct, spring break). Less lead time needed in winter.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Great base camp in any season. AC in summer, heated in winter.

name: Cliffrose Springdale
location: Springdale, riverside — five acres of gardens on the Virgin River
highlights: Five acres of gardens on the Virgin River
Heated pools year-round
Anthera restaurant on-site
Five Petals Spa — riverfront treatments
Walking distance to park and Springdale restaurants
bookingWindow: 2–4 months ahead for peak season. Popular with returning guests.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The riverside grounds are magical in fall (cottonwood color) and spring (wildflowers).

name: Flanigan's Resort & Spa
highlights: Park lodge with Deep Canyon Spa
Spotted Dog restaurant on-site
Hillside yoga with sound bath overlooking Zion
Best wellness integration in Springdale
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Check schedule for sound bath and yoga sessions.

name: Under Canvas Zion
location: Virgin, UT — 196 acres, ~30 min drive from park
highlights: Safari-style luxury tents on 196 acres
DarkSky certified site
Stargazer tents with sky windows above your bed
No WiFi — by design
Communal fire pits and curated programming
bookingWindow: 3–6 months ahead, especially for Sep–Oct. Sells out for fall equinox window.
seasonalNotes: Seasonal: typically March–November. Closed in winter. No AC — fans only in summer (can be hot June–Aug).

name: Open Sky Zion
highlights: Private and immersive luxury glamping
Farm-to-table at Black Sage restaurant
Wellness woven into every element
Multi-day wellness programs available
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The wellness programming is a genuine differentiator — not an add-on.

name: Zion Lodge
location: Inside Zion National Park — the only in-park lodge
highlights: The only lodge inside the park
Historic property with unmatched location
Wake up on the canyon floor
No driving or shuttle needed — you're already there
bookingWindow: 6–12 months ahead. Extremely limited availability. Book as early as possible.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Winter is quieter and easier to book. Summer sells out far in advance.

name: Amangiri
location: Canyon Point, UT — 90 minutes from Zion
highlights: 34 modernist suites on 900 acres
Camp Sarika with private plunge pools
Aman Spa with Navajo healing traditions
Via Ferrata climbing experience
One of the most extraordinary hotels in the American West
bookingWindow: 3–6 months ahead. Camp Sarika books further out.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Spring and fall are ideal. The desert light here is extraordinary in every season.

name: The Lodge at Bryce Canyon
highlights: The only lodging inside the park
Historic 1920s lodge with western cabins
Gas fireplaces, private porches
No TV or WiFi by design
Steps from the rim and trailheads
bookingWindow: 3–6 months ahead for summer. Book as early as possible.
seasonalNotes: Seasonal — typically April through October. The most atmospheric stay in the Bryce corridor.

name: Clear Sky Resorts
location: Cannonville, UT — Scenic Byway 12, 15 min from Bryce Canyon
highlights: Geodesic Sky Domes with panoramic glass walls
Unobstructed stargazing from your bed
Private canyon setting on Scenic Byway 12
One of the best astrotourism stays in the country
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Winter stargazing from the heated domes is exceptional.

name: Capitol Reef Resort
location: Torrey, UT — 1 mile from Capitol Reef National Park
highlights: 58 acres one mile from the park entrance
Luxury cabins with red cliff views and spa bathrooms
Glamping options include teepees and Conestoga wagons
On-site restaurant, pool, and llama hikes
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The teepees and wagons are seasonal — check availability.

name: Skyview Hotel
location: Torrey, UT — first Dark Sky designated community in Utah
highlights: 14 rooms and 6 glamping domes
Rooftop stargazing in Utah's first Dark Sky community
Minimalist design, genuinely remote feel
The best boutique option in Torrey
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The dark sky designation is real — exceptional stargazing.
